text.title: Requirements Documentation Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the requirements-documentation methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Step 1 (compose br): Draft BR (the why); reject solution-shaped phrasing.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the compose br gate.

rule.statement: Step 2 (derive sh): Per stakeholder group, derive SH records.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the derive sh gate.

rule.statement: Step 3 (decompose sr): Decompose into SR (FR + NFR); add acceptance criteria.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the decompose sr gate.

rule.statement: Step 4 (lint): Frontmatter, glossary links, AC presence, source citation count.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the lint gate.

rule.statement: Step 5 (commit): Commit to version control; tag baseline.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the commit gate.

rule.statement: If preconditions for requirements-documentation do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
